Sky Protocol hits record $419M annualized gross revenue –
Link
Sky Protocol's annualized gross revenue run rate reached a record $419.08 million in June 2026. Cumulative yield to sUSDS holders exceeded $250 million, and Sky Reserves grew to $82.5 million, up $33.7 million since March.
Matters because:
The numbers point to strong DeFi fundamentals and sustainable yield generation.

tweet.text:
Sky Protocol's Annualized Gross Revenue Run Rate Hits Record $419M

Sky Frontier Foundation said in its June 2026 financial and operational update that Sky Protocol's annualized gross revenue run rate reached a record $419.08 million, based on the last three Monthly Settlement Cycles. Cumulative yield accrued to sUSDS holders through the Sky Savings Rate has surpassed $250 million since inception, while Sky Reserves closed June at $82.5 million, up $33.7 million since March. Grove, one of Sky's Prime Agents, launched its GROVE governance token in June. Separately, a fixed-yield product for sUSDS introduced through http://Sky.Money and Pendle Finance reached $44.1 million in TVL during its first month.

AI’s Biggest Winners Have the Lowest Margins
Low-margin businesses like manufacturers and logistics firms will gain the most from AI by slashing coordination and operational costs that crush their thin profits
The key is embedding AI agents directly into existing workflows as invisible infrastructure, bypassing employee adoption barriers to deliver real margin expansion

General-purpose humanoid robots represent a ~$26 trillion addressable market

Robinhood Chain launches with $1B+ DEX volume in first week –
Link
Robinhood Chain processed over 17 million transactions, nearly 350,000 active addresses, and more than $1 billion in cumulative DEX trading volume in its debut week. Its TVL reached $250 million, making it fifth across all networks.
Matters because:
The launch shows strong demand for new blockchain infrastructure, even if activity is currently meme-coin dominated.

EU expands MiCA regulatory scope to cover tokenization and non-EU stablecoin issuers –
Link
The European Commission is seeking feedback, with a September 30 deadline, on expanding MiCA to include tokenized assets and non-EU stablecoin issuers. MiCA's grandfathering period ended July 1.
Matters because:
The move signals broader EU crypto oversight and likely higher compliance costs for platforms.

overview of the thesis and investments made by theory ventures
https://x.com/ttunguz/status/2075629199252144550

tweet.text:
Three years ago, we launched Theory Ventures with a simple premise : AI would reshape how software is built, sold, deployed, & operated. Within that world, we would build a concentrated, thesis-driven firm.

The market moved faster than even the most bullish expectations after the ChatGPT moment. Frontier models leapt from delicate demos to production systems. Open source models have become substitutes for enterprise workloads. Inference emerged as the dominant market in AI.

Underpinning all of this, AI compresses time. New models are released every 41 days. Companies reach $100m in revenue in record time. We all achieve more faster.

In celebration of our anniversary, we wanted to trace that mechanism through the market shifts of the last three years.

The first casualty of compressed time is the old language of venture capital. Seed, Series A, Series B categories still exist, but they describe the financial product companies seek rather than rather than company maturity. Venture fir

recent_posts.text: https://x.com/oyhsu/status/2075663181545087030

tweet.text:
A review of patterns we've observed in physical AI over the last 2-3 years, as the field has continued to make technical progress and aggregate talent and capital across new and existing companies: 

In 2023-2024, we started noticing more teams building for a given layer of the robotics stack. Whereas historically teams built all their own infrastructure, the promise of scaling laws for robotics meant generality was on the table, and what generality enables is for teams to build on top of a common platform — a huge advantage given the long tail nature of the physical world. We also posited that given the variance of the physical world, the proliferation of such a platform might resemble more of an open ecosystem in the vein of Android.  

This dynamic could kick off an apps>infrastructure cycle that robotics has historically lacked, but every other major technology cycle has seen (web, mobile, crypto, etc.). This is partially because there has been no breakout robotics app, but maybe,

recent_posts.text: https://x.com/dee_bosa/status/2075659013589733788

tweet.text:
Is compute scarcity masking the real economics of AI?

interesting point from @peterfenton: open-weight models are still complex to run well. For now, the big labs have the bundle: model + compute + reliability + access... and thus pricing power.

But if compute supply loosens and open model tooling gets easier, routine AI work can more easily move to cheaper/open models.

Bitcoin ETF inflows resume; July 10 saw $90.4M net inflow –
Link
After Thursday's $95.3 million outflow, Bitcoin spot ETFs posted a $90.4 million net inflow on July 10. BlackRock's IBIT led with $86.8 million, while Ethereum ETFs saw $18.4 million of inflows.
Matters because:
The reversal suggests renewed institutional demand and may help support price stability above $63,000.

Daily news snapshot: Altcoins watchlist
1. Arbitrum (ARB): +20%
• Market cap: ≈$3.5 billion
• ARB surged about 20% on July 10 after Robinhood Chain launched using Arbitrum as its underlying technology provider.
Catalysts:
• Robinhood Chain will allocate 10% of its protocol net revenue back to the Arbitrum ecosystem.
• The move highlights ecosystem expansion and revenue-sharing mechanics.
• Current activity remains primarily meme-coin driven.
Matters because:
The Robinhood Chain tie-in gives Arbitrum a clear ecosystem catalyst, though the current usage mix is still largely speculative.
2. Cash Cat (CASHCAT): +40% intraday
• Market cap: $180 million
• The meme coin briefly hit an all-time high market cap of $180 million.
Catalysts:
• Robinhood Chain's explosive first week with $1 billion+ in DEX volume.
Matters because:
It shows how quickly speculative meme-coin activity can accelerate on a new chain, but the move lacks fundamental backing.
3. Solana (SOL): ETF inflow of $150,500
• Market cap: ≈$70 billion
• Solana spot ETF (TSOL) recorded a net inflow of $150,500 on July 10, with total SOL spot ETF assets at $901 million.
Matters because:
Even a modest inflow points to continued institutional interest in Solana despite broader caution.
